2606.30335.title: BayesEvolve: Explicit Belief States for Autonomous Scientific Discovery
2606.30335.paper_summary: Autonomous scientific discovery systems increasingly use large language models (LLMs) to propose new hypotheses, but many such systems condition primarily on experimental memory: archives of high-scoring candidates or heuristic summaries of recent trials. We argue that discovery agents should instead maintain explicit, uncertainty-aware beliefs about hypothesis quality. We introduce BayesEvolve, a belief-guided discovery framework that converts experimental evidence into a predictive belief state and uses this belief to guide future experimentation. As a controlled testbed for belief-guided discovery, we evaluate BayesEvolve on shifted BBOB-style black-box optimization tasks, leaving program and laboratory discovery domains to future work. BayesEvolve improves sample efficiency over memory- and archive-guided LLM baselines under a fixed evaluation budget. We further show that the belief state is predictive on held-out candidate pools, that controlled decision-rule ablations favor belief-guided selection with an annealed uncertainty bonus, and that BayesEvolve exhibits productive late-stage concentration rather than unfocused exploration.
2606.30335.paper_summary_zh: 自主科學發現系統越來越多地使用大型語言模型 (LLMs) 來提出新的假設，但許多這樣的系統主要依賴於實驗記憶：高分候選者的檔案或近期試驗的啟發式摘要。<lb/>我們認為，發現代理應該保持對假設質量的明確、不確定性意識的信念。<lb/>我們介紹了 BayesEvolve，一個基於信念的發現框架，將實驗證據轉換為預測信念狀態，並利用這種信念來指導未來的實驗。<lb/>作為基於信念的發現的受控測試平台，我們在轉移的 BBOB 風格黑箱優化任務上評估 BayesEvolve，將程序和實驗室發現領域留待未來的工作。<lb/>在固定的評估預算下，BayesEvolve 在樣本效率上超越了基於記憶和檔案的 LLM 基準。<lb/>我們進一步顯示，信念狀態在保留的候選池上具有預測性，受控的決策規則消融偏好基於信念的選擇，並且帶有退火不確定性獎勵，BayesEvolve 表現出有效的晚期集中，而不是無焦點的探索。
2606.30335.author: Xuening Wu et.al.
2606.30335.authors: Xuening Wu, Shan Yu, Qianya Xu, Shenqin Yin
